mapping.a_element: quantum sine-Gordon (SG) model describing the low-energy excitations of KCuGaF6 in a magnetic field
mapping.b_element: sine-Gordon-massive Thirring (sG-mT) model in a periodic box

mapping.a_element: staggered magnetic field h induced perpendicular to external field H, parameterized by c_s = h/H
mapping.b_element: external magnetic field h_z in the XXZ chain or the periodic box size in the sG-mT model

mapping.a_element: resonance modes (solitons, breathers, interbreather transitions) described by quantum SG field theory
mapping.b_element: finite size corrections and thermodynamic quantities derived from the non-linear integral equation (NLIE) for the sG-mT model

mapping.a_element: adjustable parameter c_s = h/H governing the resonance conditions
mapping.b_element: parameters in the NLIE governing the free energy and excitations in the sG-mT model

brief_justification: Both papers employ the quantum sine-Gordon model as the underlying mathematical framework: Paper A uses it to describe elementary excitations in a 1D antiferromagnet, while Paper B derives a unified non-linear integral equation (NLIE) for the sine-Gordon-massive Thirring model to compute thermodynamic properties. The core machinery—solving the sine-Gordon model's dynamics or thermodynamics—is shared, with role-for-role correspondences in parameters and excitations.
